steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The decisive clue is the opening pivot: a common problem (weeds) is immediately answered with a "surprising solution" (eating them). The word "surprising" signals that unexpectedness is central to the text's purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To suggest an unexpected solution to a problem.: Matches the structure exactly — the passage frames weed removal as the problem and eating weeds as the surprising answer. ✓
  • Choice B — To discuss the best way to garden.: The passage never compares gardening methods or recommends any gardening technique; gardening is only the backdrop for the problem. ✗
  • Choice C — To describe how good some plants taste.: Flavor is mentioned only in the final sentence as a secondary detail, not the main point; the passage is not a review of plant taste. ✗
  • Choice D — To identify a new cooking trend.: No trend is identified or discussed; the passage proposes a solution to a specific problem rather than describing what people are currently doing. ✗
That is the correct answer.
